name: Healthy Brain Network Biobank (HBN)
description: An ongoing Child Mind Institute initiative building an open biobank from a target of 10,000 New York-area participants aged 5–21. Each participant contributes psychiatric, behavioral, cognitive and lifestyle phenotyping plus multimodal brain imaging (resting and naturalistic-viewing fMRI, diffusion and morphometric MRI), EEG, eye-tracking, voice/video, genetics and actigraphy — a transdiagnostic resource for developmental and social neuroscience.
citation: Alexander LM, Escalera J, Ai L, et al. (2017). An open resource for transdiagnostic research in pediatric mental health and learning disorders: the Healthy Brain Network. Scientific Data, 4, 170181.

publications.title: HBN-EEG: The FAIR implementation of the Healthy Brain Network electroencephalography dataset
